segments.text: And Abraham was old, and well stricken in age: and the LORD had blessed Abraham in all things.

footnotes: well stricken in age: Heb. gone into days

segments.text: And Abraham said unto his eldest servant of his house, that ruled over all that he had,
Put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh:
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Unto thy seed will I give this land; he shall send his angel before thee, and thou shalt take a wife unto my son from thence.
segments.speaker: The LORD God

segments.text: O LORD God of my master Abraham, I pray thee, send me good speed this day, and shew kindness unto my master Abraham.
segments.speaker: Abraham's Servant

segments.text: Blessed be the LORD God of my master Abraham, who hath not left destitute my master of his mercy and his truth: I being in the way, the LORD led me to the house of my master's brethren.
segments.speaker: Abraham's Servant

segments.text: The thing proceedeth from the LORD: we cannot speak unto thee bad or good.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Thou art our sister, be thou the mother of thousands of millions, and let thy seed possess the gate of those which hate them.
segments.speaker: Rebekah's Family

segments.text: And Isaac brought her into his mother Sarah's tent, and took Rebekah, and she became his wife; and he loved her: and Isaac was comforted after his mother's death.
